description:
# fz-NA: nil, true, false are atoms

## The thing in one sentence

Eliminate the lattice/runtime inconsistency where the typer + reducer
treat `Const::True`/`Const::False` as `atom_lit("true")`/`atom_lit("false")`
in their Descrs while the runtime emits them as a distinct `TAG_SPECIAL`
bit pattern. Make `nil`, `true`, `false` real atoms (with reserved
compile-time atom IDs 0, 1, 2). The lattice and runtime agree;
the `TAG_SPECIAL` tag class goes away.

## Why now

Surfaced by the lurkers audit after fz-s9y. Pattern: the reducer's
`fold_const` maps `Const::True â†’ Descr::atom_lit("true")` (atoms
axis) while codegen emits `TRUE_BITS = SPECIAL_TRUE` (special-tagged).
If the reducer's fold reasons about a `Const::True ==
Pattern::Atom("true")` comparison, it'd say "equal" â€” but at runtime
they're different bit patterns and `case x do true -> a; :true -> b end`
has both clauses reachable for different inputs. Same shape as fz-s9y
(lattice and runtime telling different stories).

## Public API preserved

`NIL_BITS`, `TRUE_BITS`, `FALSE_BITS` stay as named constants; their
*definitions* shift from special-tagged to atom-tagged. Most of the
~32 references don't move. `FzValue::is_nil`/`is_true`/`is_false`
keep their signatures; implementations swap to atom-id checks.
`Descr::nil()` and `Descr::bool_t()` keep their signatures; impls
switch to atoms-axis views.

## Concrete acceptance

  - Atom IDs 0/1/2 reserved at compile-time for "nil"/"true"/"false".
  - `nil == :nil` â‰¡ true. `true == :true` â‰¡ true. Same value.
  - `[] != nil` (preserves fz-s9y).
  - `print(:nil)` shows "nil" (not ":nil"). Same for :true, :false.
  - `BasicBits::NIL` and `BasicBits::BOOL` axes gone.
  - `SPECIAL_NIL`/`SPECIAL_TRUE`/`SPECIAL_FALSE` constants gone.
  - `TAG_SPECIAL` tag class gone (empty after the move).
  - All 849 tests pass plus the new pinning tests.

## Out of scope

  - Eliminating `Pattern::Nil` / `Pattern::Bool` / `Const::Nil`/`True`/
    `False` AST variants. They become parse-time sugar that lowers
    identically to atom literals; the variants stay for source-level
    convenience. Future ticket if we want them removed.

## DAG

```
NA.1  Runtime: atom IDs reserved; *_BITS redefined as atom-tagged.
        â†“
NA.2  Type system: Descr::nil()/bool_t() switch to atom-axis views;
       BasicBits::NIL/BOOL removed.
        â†“
NA.3  Pretty-print: atoms with reserved IDs render without colon.
        â†“
NA.4  Sweep, stale-comment fix, pinning tests.
```

Strictly linear.

## Removals are first-class

  - SPECIAL_NIL, SPECIAL_TRUE, SPECIAL_FALSE constants.
  - TAG_SPECIAL tag class (becomes empty / reserved).
  - BasicBits::NIL, BasicBits::BOOL axes.
  - The lattice/runtime inconsistency surfaced in the post-fz-s9y
    lurkers audit.
  - Stale ir_typer.rs:22 "list_of(elem) âˆª nil" comment.
title: [EPIC] nil/true/false are atoms (BEAM-conform)
